dtcs.description: Driver Side Solar Sensor Short Circuit The solar sensor (automatic light control sensor) is installed on the upper side of the instrument panel. It detects sunlight and creates a signal that is used for air conditioning control in AUTO mode. The output voltage from the solar sensor (automatic light control sensor) varies in accordance with the amount of sunlight. When the amount of sunlight increases, the output voltage increases. As the sunlight decreases, the output voltage decreases. The air conditioning amplifier assembly detects changes in the output voltage from the solar sensor (automatic light control sensor). PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: READ VALUE USING TECHSTREAM (a) Connect the Techstream to the DLC3. (b) Turn the power switch on (IG). (c) Turn the Techstream on. (d) Enter the following menus: Body Electrical / Air Conditioner / Data List. (e) Check the value(s) by referring to the table below. Air Conditioner OK: The display is as specified in the Normal Condition column. Result: B --  PROCEED TO NEXT SUSPECTED AREA SHOWN IN PROBLEM SYMPTOMS TABLE  See: Heating and Air Conditioning/Testing and Inspection/Symptom Related Diagnostic Procedures C --  REPLACE AIR CONDITIONING AMPLIFIER ASSEMBLY  See: Heating and Air Conditioning/Control Module HVAC/Service and Repair/Removal A -- Continue to next step.

CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (POWER SOURCE CIRCUIT) (a) Disconnect the solar sensor (automatic light control sensor) connector. (b) Measure the voltage according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Voltage: Text in Illustration NG --  CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (MAIN BODY ECU (MULTIPLEX NETWORK BODY ECU) - SOLAR SENSOR) OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (SOLAR SENSOR (AUTOMATIC LIGHT CONTROL SENSOR) - AIR CONDITIONING AMPLIFIER ASSEMBLY) (a) Disconnect the solar sensor (automatic light control sensor) connector. (b) Disconnect the air conditioning amplifier assembly connector. (c) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Resistance: NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR OK -- Continue to next step.

INSPECT SOLAR SENSOR (AUTOMATIC LIGHT CONTROL SENSOR) (a) Remove the solar sensor (automatic light control sensor)    See: Heating and Air Conditioning/Sensors and Switches - HVAC/Solar Sensor/Service and Repair/Removal. (b) Reconnect the solar sensor (automatic light control sensor) connector. (c) Turn the power switch on (IG). (d) Measure the voltage according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Voltage: NOTICE: * The connection procedure for using a digital tester such as a TOYOTA electrical tester is shown above. When using an analog tester, connect the negative (-) lead to terminal 1 and the positive (+) lead to terminal 6 of the solar sensor (automatic light control sensor). * While using the auxiliary battery during inspection, do not bring the positive and negative tester probes too close to each other as a short circuit may occur. HINT * Use an incandescent light for inspection. Bring it within about 30 cm (11.8 in.) of the solar sensor (automatic light control sensor). * As the inspection light is moved away from the sensor, the voltage decreases. Text in Illustration NG --  REPLACE SOLAR SENSOR (AUTOMATIC LIGHT CONTROL SENSOR)  See: Heating and Air Conditioning/Sensors and Switches - HVAC/Solar Sensor/Service and Repair/Removal OK --  REPLACE AIR CONDITIONING AMPLIFIER ASSEMBLY  See: Heating and Air Conditioning/Control Module HVAC/Service and Repair/Removal

dtcs.description: Telematics Transceiver Malfunction This DTC is set when an error in the EEPROM or PLL IC is detected on the DCM (Telematics Transceiver) self-check. The EEPROM (Electrically Erasable Programmable Read-Only Memory) stores the various data to operate the Safety Connect system. The PLL IC (Phase-Locked Loop Integrated Circuit) oscillates telephone radio frequency. NOTICE: Regardless of whether DTC B15A8 has been output as a present or history DTC, the DCM (Telematics Transceiver) should be replaced. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: REPLACE DCM (TELEMATICS TRANSCEIVER) (a) Replace the DCM (Telematics Transceiver)    See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Emergency Contact Module/Service and Repair/Removal. NOTICE: * The power switch must be off. * Do not swap the DCM (Telematics Transceiver) with one from another vehicle. NEXT --  PERFORM DCM ACTIVATION  See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Emergency Contact Module/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/DCM Activation Navigation System (for Navigation Receiver Type) NAVIGATION / MULTI INFO DISPLAY: NAVIGATION SYSTEM (for Navigation Receiver Type): B1551, B15A0, B15AD, B15B0, B15B3, B15B5, B15B7, B15BA: HD Radio Tuner Malfunction

dtcs.description: Telematics Transceiver Antenna Disconnected This DTC is set when the DCM (Telematics Transceiver) detects an open or a short in the telephone and GPS antenna circuit. The DCM (Telematics Transceiver) oscillates and receives 824 - 894 MHz or 1850 - 1990 MHz radio-frequency through the telephone and GPS antenna assembly. HINT Refer to "PARTS LOCATION" for the installation location of telephone and GPS antenna cord See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Emergency Contact Module/Locations/Safety Connect System. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CHECK DTC (a) Turn the power switch off. (b) Connect the Techstream to the DLC3. (c) Turn the power switch on (IG) and wait for 10 seconds. (d) Turn the Techstream on. (e) Perform "HEALTH CHECK" and check for current DTCs    See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Emergency Contact Module/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/Health Check. HINT Refer to "PARTS LOCATION" for the installation location of telephone and GPS antenna cord See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Emergency Contact Module/Locations/Safety Connect System. PROCEDURE

dtcs.description: Speed Signal Malfunction The navigation ECU sub-assembly receives a vehicle speed signal from the No. 3 meter circuit plate and information from the navigation antenna assembly, and then adjusts the vehicle position. The navigation ECU sub-assembly stores this DTC when the difference between the speed information that the navigation antenna assembly receives and the SPD pulse received from the No. 3 meter circuit plate becomes large. HINT * A voltage of 12 V or 5 V is output from each ECU and then input to the No. 3 meter circuit plate. The signal is changed to a pulse signal at the transistor in the No. 3 meter circuit plate. Each ECU controls the respective systems based on the pulse signal. * If a short occurs in any of the ECUs or in the wire harness connected to an ECU, all systems in the diagram below will not operate normally. NOTICE: After replacing the navigation ECU sub-assembly of vehicles subscribed to pay-type satellite radio broadcasts, registration of the XM radio ID is necessary (w/ SDARS System). PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CHECK VEHICLE SENSOR (OPERATION CHECK) (a) Enter the "Vehicle Sensors" screen. Refer to Check GPS & Vehicle Sensors in Operation Check    See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Navigation System/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/Navigation System (for Navigation Receiver Type)/Operation Check. (b) While driving the vehicle, compare the "Speed" indicator to the reading on the speedometer. Check if these readings are almost equal. HINT The No. 3 meter circuit plate receives the vehicle speed signal from the skid control ECU via CAN communication. Therefore, perform the following inspection referring to values on the Data List of the skid control ECU because it is the source of the vehicle speed signal. OK: Vehicle speed displayed on the "Vehicle Sensors" screen is almost the same as the actual vehicle speed measured using the Techstream    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Scan Tool Testing and Procedures/Data List / Active Test. NG --  INSPECT NO. 3 METER CIRCUIT PLATE (OUTPUT WAVEFORM) OK --  REPLACE NAVIGATION ECU SUB-ASSEMBLY  See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Navigation System/Navigation Module/Service and Repair/Removal

INSPECT NO. 3 METER CIRCUIT PLATE (OUTPUT WAVEFORM) (a) Check the output waveform. (1) Remove the No. 3 meter circuit plate with the connector(s) still connected. (2) Connect an oscilloscope to terminal L27-7 (+S) and body ground. (3) Turn the power switch on (IG). (4) Turn a wheel slowly. (5) Check the signal waveform according to the condition(s) in the table below. OK: The waveform is similar to that shown in the illustration. HINT When the system is functioning normally, one wheel revolution generates 4 pulses. As the vehicle speed increases, the width indicated by (A) in the illustration narrows. Text in Illustration NG --  GO TO METER / GAUGE SYSTEM See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Testing and Inspection/Component Tests and General Diagnostics/Speed Signal Circuit OK -- Continue to next step.

INSPECT NAVIGATION ECU SUB-ASSEMBLY (INPUT WAVEFORM) (a) Check the input waveform. (1) Remove the navigation ECU sub-assembly with the connector(s) still connected. (2) Connect an oscilloscope to terminal L145-17 (SPD) and body ground. (3) Turn the power switch on (IG). (4) Turn a wheel slowly. (5) Check the signal waveform according to the condition(s) in the table below. OK: The waveform is similar to that shown in the illustration. HINT When the system is functioning normally, one wheel revolution generates 4 pulses. As the vehicle speed increases, the width indicated by (A) in the illustration narrows. Text in Illustration NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR (NAVIGATION ECU SUB-ASSEMBLY - NO. 3 METER CIRCUIT PLATE) OK --  REPLACE NAVIGATION ECU SUB-ASSEMBLY  See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Navigation System/Navigation Module/Service and Repair/Removal Navigation System (for Navigation Receiver Type)

dtcs.description: Airbag Signal Malfunction/Not Input If the DCM (Telematics Transceiver) detects an error in communication between the DCM (Telematics Transceiver) and the center airbag sensor assembly as a result of the DCM (Telematics Transceiver) self check, this DTC will be set. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CHECK DTC (AIRBAG SYSTEM) (a) Turn the power switch off. (b) Connect the Techstream to the DLC3. (c) Turn the power switch on (IG) and wait for 10 seconds. (d) Turn the Techstream on. (e) Check the DTC of "AIRBAG SYSTEM"    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes/DTC Check / Clear. Result: B --  GO TO AIRBAG SYSTEM  See: Diagnostic Trouble Code Descriptions/Airbag System A -- Continue to next step.

INSPECT DCM (TELEMATICS TRANSCEIVER) (GSW SIGNAL) (a) Remove the DCM (Telematics Transceiver) but do not disconnect the connectors    See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Emergency Contact Module/Service and Repair/Removal. (b) Measure the voltage according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Voltage: Text in Illustration Result: Reference Waveform B --  CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (FOR SHORT CIRCUIT) C --  REPLACE DCM (TELEMATICS TRANSCEIVER) A -- Continue to next step.

CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (FOR OPEN CIRCUIT) (a) Remove the center airbag sensor assembly but do not disconnect the connectors    See: Relays and Modules/Relays and Modules - Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Control Module/Service and Repair/Removal (b) Measure the voltage according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Voltage: Text in Illustration Reference Waveform NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR OK --  REPLACE CENTER AIRBAG SENSOR ASSEMBLY  See: Relays and Modules/Relays and Modules - Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Control Module/Service and Repair/Removal

CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (FOR SHORT CIRCUIT) (a) Disconnect the L160 DCM (Telematics Transceiver) connector. (b) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Resistance: Text in Illustration NG --  CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (DCM (TELEMATICS TRANSCEIVER) - CENTER AIRBAG SENSOR ASSEMBLY) OK -- Continue to next step.

REPLACE DCM (TELEMATICS TRANSCEIVER) (a) Replace the DCM (Telematics Transceiver)    See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Emergency Contact Module/Service and Repair/Removal. NOTICE: * The power switch must be off. * Do not swap the DCM (Telematics Transceiver) with one from another vehicle. NEXT --  PERFORM DCM ACTIVATION  See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Emergency Contact Module/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/DCM Activation

CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (DCM (TELEMATICS TRANSCEIVER) - CENTER AIRBAG SENSOR ASSEMBLY) (a) Disconnect the L160 DCM (Telematics Transceiver) connector. (b) Disconnect the L16 center airbag sensor connector. (c) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Resistance: NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR OK --  REPLACE CENTER AIRBAG SENSOR ASSEMBLY  See: Relays and Modules/Relays and Modules - Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Control Module/Service and Repair/Removal

dtcs.description: Manual Button Malfunction This DTC is set when the DCM (Telematics Transceiver) detects an open or short circuit in the manual (SOS) switch. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: READ VALUE USING TECHSTREAM (MANUAL (SOS) SWITCH OPERATION) (a) Turn the power switch off. (b) Connect the Techstream to the DLC3. (c) Turn the power switch on (IG) and wait for 10 seconds. (d) Turn the Techstream on. (e) Enter the following menus: Body Electrical / Telematics / Data List. (f) Check that the manual (SOS) switch condition observed on the Techstream changes according to manual (SOS) switch operation. Telematics (DCM (Telematics Transceiver) *: Pressing the manual (SOS) switch will connect the call center. Result: B --  INSPECT DCM (TELEMATICS TRANSCEIVER) (SIG1 VOLTAGE) A -- Continue to next step.

REPLACE DCM (TELEMATICS TRANSCEIVER) (a) Replace the DCM (Telematics Transceiver)    See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Emergency Contact Module/Service and Repair/Removal. NOTICE: * The power switch must be off. * Do not swap the DCM (Telematics Transceiver) with one from another vehicle. NEXT --  PERFORM DCM ACTIVATION  See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Emergency Contact Module/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/DCM Activation

INSPECT DCM (TELEMATICS TRANSCEIVER) (SIG1 VOLTAGE) (a) Remove the DCM (Telematics Transceiver) but do not disconnect the connectors    See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Emergency Contact Module/Service and Repair/Removal. NEXT --  PERFORM DCM ACTIVATION  See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Emergency Contact Module/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/DCM Activation

CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (DCM (TELEMATICS TRANSCEIVER) - MAP LIGHT ASSEMBLY) (a) Disconnect the L160 DCM (Telematics Transceiver) connector. (b) Disconnect the U3 map light assembly connector. (c) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Resistance: NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR OK -- Continue to next step.

NEXT --  PERFORM DCM ACTIVATION  See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Emergency Contact Module/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/DCM Activation Navigation System (for Navigation Receiver Type)

dtcs.description: Stereo Component Amplifier Disconnected The navigation ECU sub-assembly and stereo component amplifier assembly are connected by the AVC-LAN communication line. When an AVC-LAN communication error occurs between the navigation ECU sub-assembly and stereo component amplifier assembly, this DTC will be stored. HINT * Even if no fault is present, this DTC may be stored depending on the auxiliary battery condition or engine start voltage. * The navigation ECU sub-assembly is the master unit. NOTICE: * Inspect the fuses for circuits related to this system before performing the following inspection procedure. * After replacing the navigation ECU sub-assembly of vehicles subscribed to pay-type satellite radio broadcasts, registration of the XM radio ID is necessary (w/ SDARS System). PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CHECK DTC (a) If DTC B15C3 is output, perform the troubleshooting of DTC B15C3 first. Result: B --  GO TO DTC "B15C3" IN DIAGNOSTIC TROUBLE CODE CHART  See: Diagnostic Trouble Code Descriptions/Navigation System (for Navigation Receiver Type) A -- Continue to next step.

CHECK OPTIONAL COMPONENTS (INCLUDING ASSOCIATED WIRING) (a) Check that optional components (including associated wiring) which generate radio waves are not installed. Result: HINT * Electrical noise from radio waves generated by optional components or the wiring for those components may affect AVC-LAN communication. * This DTC may be stored when an AVC-LAN communication error occurs due to electrical noise. B --  CHECK DTC A -- Continue to next step.

REMOVE OPTIONAL COMPONENTS (INCLUDING ASSOCIATED WIRING) (a) Remove optional components (including associated wiring). NOTICE: Do not remove optional components or associated wiring without the permission of the customer. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

CHECK DTC (a) Clear the DTCs    See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Navigation System/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes/Navigation System (for Navigation Receiver Type). (b) Recheck for DTCs and check if the same DTC is output again. OK: No DTCs are output. NG --  CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (STEREO COMPONENT AMPLIFIER ASSEMBLY - AUXILIARY BATTERY, BODY GROUND) OK --  END

CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (STEREO COMPONENT AMPLIFIER ASSEMBLY - AUXILIARY BATTERY, BODY GROUND) (a) Disconnect the stereo component amplifier assembly connectors. (b) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Resistance: (c) Measure the voltage according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Voltage: NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (NAVIGATION ECU SUB-ASSEMBLY - STEREO COMPONENT AMPLIFIER ASSEMBLY) (a) Disconnect the navigation ECU sub-assembly connector. (b) Disconnect the stereo component amplifier assembly connector. (c) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Resistance: NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR OK -- Continue to next step.

REPLACE STEREO COMPONENT AMPLIFIER ASSEMBLY (a) Replace the stereo component amplifier assembly    See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Radio, Stereo, and Compact Disc/Amplifier/Service and Repair/Removal. (b) Clear the DTCs    See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Navigation System/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes/Navigation System (for Navigation Receiver Type). (c) Recheck for DTCs and check if the same DTC is output again. OK: No DTCs are output. NG --  REPLACE NAVIGATION ECU SUB-ASSEMBLY  See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Navigation System/Navigation Module/Service and Repair/Removal OK --  END Navigation System (for Radio and Display Type)

dtcs.description: Backup Battery Degradation This DTC is set when the DCM (Telematics Transceiver) detects either of the following conditions. * The BUB (Back-Up Battery) charge level becomes less than the criteria. * Automatic Collision Notification (ACN) occurred. When this DTC is set, the BUB (Back-Up Battery) must be replaced. BUB (Back-Up Battery) degradation DTC B15EC is not reported in deactive mode. NOTICE: * If this DTC is set, be certain to replace the BUB (Back-Up Battery) with a new one. * After the BUB (Back-Up Battery) is replaced, it is necessary to clear this DTC. * The BUB (Back-Up Battery) must not be replaced while an ACN call is in progress. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CHECK DTC (a) Turn the power switch off. (b) Connect the Techstream to the DLC3. (c) Turn the power switch on (IG) and wait for 10 seconds. (d) Turn the Techstream on. (e) Perform "HEALTH CHECK" and check for current DTCs    See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Emergency Contact Module/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/Health Check. Result: B --  CHECK FOR INTERMITTENT PROBLEMS  See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antitheft and Alarm Systems/Diagrams/Diagnostic Aids/How To Troubleshoot ECU Controlled Systems/How To Proceed With Troubleshooting A --  REPLACE BUB (BACK-UP BATTERY)  See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Emergency Battery/Service and Repair/Removal

dtcs.description: Harddisk Reading/Writing Malfunction(Low Temperature)

Harddisk Reading/Writing Malfunction(High Temperature) These DTCs are stored when the temperature is higher or lower than the operation range of the hard disk drive built into the navigation ECU sub-assembly, and hard disk drive read/write errors occur more than a specified number of times. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CHECK DTC (a) Clear the DTCs    See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Navigation System/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes/Navigation System (for Navigation Receiver Type). (b) Recheck for DTCs and check if the same DTC is output again. OK: No DTCs are output. NG --  REPLACE HARD DISK DRIVE  See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Navigation System/Navigation Display/Service and Repair/Removal OK --  USE SIMULATION METHOD TO CHECK  See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antitheft and Alarm Systems/Diagrams/Diagnostic Aids/How To Troubleshoot ECU Controlled Systems/How To Proceed With Troubleshooting

dtcs.description: XM Tuner Antenna Disconnected

XM Tuner Antenna Short These DTCs are stored when a malfunction occurs in the amplifier antenna assembly (for Roof) which is connected to the radio and display receiver assembly. NOTICE: After replacing the radio and display receiver assembly of vehicles subscribed to pay-type satellite radio broadcasts, XM radio ID registration is necessary. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: REPLACE ANTENNA CORD SUB-ASSEMBLY (a) Replace the antenna cord sub-assembly    See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antenna/Antenna, Radio/Antenna Cable/Service and Repair/Removal. (b) Clear the DTCs    See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Navigation System/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes/Navigation System (for Navigation Receiver Type). (c) Recheck for DTCs and check if the same DTC is output again. OK: No DTCs are output. NG --  INSPECT NO. 2 ANTENNA CORD SUB-ASSEMBLY OK --  END

INSPECT NO. 2 ANTENNA CORD SUB-ASSEMBLY (a) Disconnect the No. 2 antenna cord sub-assembly. (b) Measure the resistance of the No. 2 antenna cord sub-assembly to check for an open circuit in the No. 2 antenna cord sub-assembly. Standard Resistance: Below 1 Ohms (c) Measure the resistance between the No. 2 antenna cord sub-assembly and body ground to check for a short circuit in the No. 2 antenna cord sub-assembly. Standard Resistance: 10 kOhms or higher NG --  REPLACE NO. 2 ANTENNA CORD SUB-ASSEMBLY  See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antenna/Antenna, Radio/Antenna Cable/Service and Repair/Removal OK -- Continue to next step.

INSPECT NO. 3 ANTENNA CORD SUB-ASSEMBLY (a) Disconnect the No. 3 antenna cord sub-assembly. (b) Measure the resistance of the No. 3 antenna cord sub-assembly to check for an open circuit in the No. 3 antenna cord sub-assembly. Standard Resistance: Below 1 Ohms (c) Measure the resistance between the No. 3 antenna cord sub-assembly and body ground to check for a short circuit in the No. 3 antenna cord sub-assembly. Standard Resistance: 10 kOhms or higher NG --  REPLACE NO. 3 ANTENNA CORD SUB-ASSEMBLY  See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antenna/Antenna, Radio/Antenna Cable/Service and Repair/Removal OK -- Continue to next step.

REPLACE ROOF ANTENNA POLE SUB-ASSEMBLY (a) Replace the roof antenna pole sub-assembly    See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antenna/Antenna, Radio/Service and Repair/Removal and Replacement/Removal. (b) Clear the DTCs    See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Navigation System/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes/Navigation System (for Navigation Receiver Type). (c) Recheck for DTCs and check if the same DTC is output again. OK: No DTCs are output. NG --  REPLACE AMPLIFIER ANTENNA ASSEMBLY (for Roof) OK --  END

REPLACE AMPLIFIER ANTENNA ASSEMBLY (for Roof) (a) Replace the amplifier antenna assembly (for Roof)   See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antenna/Antenna Amplifier/Service and Repair/Removal. (b) Clear the DTCs    See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Navigation System/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes/Navigation System (for Navigation Receiver Type). (c) Recheck for DTCs and check if the same DTC is output again. OK: No DTCs are output. NG --  REPLACE NAVIGATION ECU SUB-ASSEMBLY  See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Navigation System/Navigation Module/Service and Repair/Removal OK --  END Navigation System (for Radio and Display Type)

dtcs.description: A/C ECU Vehicle Information Reading/Writing Processor Malfunction This DTC is stored when items controlled by the Air conditioning amplifier assembly cannot be customized via the navigation system vehicle customization screen. HINT The Air conditioning amplifier assembly controls the air conditioning system related items that are customizable via the multi-display screen. NOTICE: After replacing the navigation ECU sub-assembly of vehicles subscribed to pay-type satellite radio broadcasts, registration of the XM radio ID is necessary (w/ SDARS System). PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CHECK CAN COMMUNICATION SYSTEM (a) Use the Techstream to check if the CAN communication system is functioning normally    See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Information Bus/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/CAN Communication System/How to Proceed With Troubleshooting. OK: CAN communication DTCs are not output. NG --  GO TO CAN COMMUNICATION SYSTEM  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Information Bus/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/CAN Communication System/How to Proceed With OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK AIR CONDITIONING SYSTEM (a) Check if the air conditioning system is malfunctioning    See: Heating and Air Conditioning/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/How To Proceed With Troubleshooting. HINT Customization may not be possible depending on the malfunction in the air conditioning system. OK: No malfunctions are present in the air conditioning system. NG --  GO TO AIR CONDITIONING SYSTEM  See: Heating and Air Conditioning/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/How To Proceed With Troubleshooting OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK CUSTOMIZE ITEMS (a) Display the "Climate Settings" screen. (b) Make a note of the customizable items for the air conditioning system. HINT These air conditioning system items shown as customizable via the multi-display vehicle customization screen can also be changed using the Techstream. In the following steps, the Techstream will be used. (1) To match-up the items in the multi-display with the items on the Techstream, check the customize parameters list for the air conditioning system related items that can be customized via the multi-display screen    See: Heating and Air Conditioning/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning. (c) Using the Techstream, confirm that it is possible to change the settings of the air conditioning system related customize parameters that are shown as customizable via the multi-display screen. OK: The items available for customization via the multi-display screen can be customized successfully using the Techstream. NG --  REPLACE AIR CONDITIONING AMPLIFIER ASSEMBLY  See: Heating and Air Conditioning/Control Module HVAC/Service and Repair/Removal OK --  REPLACE NAVIGATION ECU SUB-ASSEMBLY  See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Navigation System/Navigation Module/Service and Repair/Removal Navigation System (for Navigation Receiver Type)

dtcs.description: Main Body ECU Vehicle Information Reading/Writing Process Malfunction This DTC is stored when items controlled by the main body ECU (multiplex network body ECU) cannot be customized via the navigation system vehicle customization screen. HINT The main body ECU (multiplex network body ECU) controls the items for the following systems that are customizable via the multi-display screen: * Power door lock control system * Wireless door lock control system * Lighting system (for Interior) * Lighting system (for Exterior) NOTICE: After replacing the navigation ECU sub-assembly of vehicles subscribed to pay-type satellite radio broadcasts, registration of the XM radio ID is necessary (w/ SDARS System). PROCEDURE /Interior Lighting System/How To Proceed With Troubleshooting OK -- Continue to next step. /Exterior Lighting System/How To Proceed With Troubleshooting OK -- Continue to next step.

OK: CAN communication DTCs are not output. NG --  GO TO WIRELESS DOOR LOCK CONTROL SYSTEM  See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antitheft and Alarm Systems/Keyless Entry/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/How To Proceed With Troubleshooting OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK LIGHTING SYSTEM (for Interior) (a) Check if the lighting system (for Interior) is malfunctioning    See: Lighting and Horns/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/Interior Lighting System/How To Proceed With Troubleshooting. HINT Customization may not be possible depending on the malfunction in the lighting system (for Interior). OK: No malfunctions are present in the lighting system (for Interior). NG --  GO TO LIGHTING SYSTEM (for Interior)  See: Lighting and Horns/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic

CHECK LIGHTING SYSTEM (for Exterior) (a) Check if the lighting system (for Exterior) is malfunctioning    See: Lighting and Horns/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/Exterior Lighting System/How To Proceed With Troubleshooting. CHECK CUSTOMIZE ITEMS (a) Display the "Door Lock Settings" or "Lights Settings" screen. (b) Make a note of the customizable items for each main body ECU (multiplex network body ECU) related system. HINT These items shown as customizable via the navigation system vehicle customization screen can also be changed using the Techstream. In a following step, the Techstream will be used. (1) To match-up the items in the multi-display with the items on the Techstream, check the customize parameters list for the power door lock control system related items that can be customized via the multi-display screen    See: Body and Frame/Locks/Power Locks/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Customize Parameters. (2) To match-up the items in the multi-display with the items on the Techstream, check the customize parameters list for the wireless door lock control system related items that can be customized via the multi-display screen    See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antitheft and Alarm Systems/Keyless Entry/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning. (3) To match-up the items in the multi-display with the items on the Techstream, check the customize parameters list for the lighting system (for Interior) related items that can be customized via the multi-display screen    See: Lighting and Horns/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Interior Lighting System. (4) To match-up the items in the multi-display with the items on the Techstream, check the customize parameters list for the lighting system (for Exterior) related items that can be customized via the multi-display screen    See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antitheft and Alarm Systems/Keyless Entry/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning. (c) Using the Techstream, confirm that it is possible to change the settings of the customize parameters that are available for customization via the multi-display screen. OK: The items available for customization via the multi-display screen can be customized successfully using the Techstream. NG --  REPLACE MAIN BODY ECU (MULTIPLEX NETWORK BODY ECU)  See: Body and Frame/Body Control Systems/Body Control Module/Service and Repair/Removal OK --  REPLACE NAVIGATION ECU SUB-ASSEMBLY  See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Navigation System/Navigation Module/Service and Repair/Removal Navigation System (for Radio and Display Type)

dtcs.description: Certification ECU Vehicle Information Reading/Writing Process Malfunction This DTC is stored when items controlled by the certification ECU (smart key ECU assembly) cannot be customized via the navigation system vehicle customization screen. HINT The certification ECU (smart key ECU assembly) controls the items for the following systems that are customizable via the multi-display screen: * Smart key system (for Entry Function)*1 * Push-button start*2 * *1: w/ Smart Key System (for Entry Function) * *2: w/o Smart Key System (for Entry Function) NOTICE: * After replacing the navigation ECU sub-assembly of vehicles subscribed to pay-type satellite radio broadcasts, registration of the XM radio ID is necessary (w/ SDARS System). * If the certification ECU (smart key ECU assembly) or ID code box (immobiliser code ECU) is replaced, refer to Registration See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antitheft and Alarm Systems/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Engine Immobilizer System. PROCEDURE

dtcs.description: Extension Module Disconnected The extension module and the radio and display receiver assembly are connected by the AVC-LAN communication line. When an AVC-LAN communication error occurs between the extension module and the radio and display receiver assembly, this DTC will be stored. HINT * *1: Even if no fault is present, this DTC may be stored depending on the auxiliary battery condition or hybrid control system start voltage. * The radio and display receiver assembly is the master unit. NOTICE: After replacing the radio and display receiver assembly of vehicles subscribed to pay-type satellite radio broadcasts, XM radio ID registration is necessary (w/ SDARS System). PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CHECK OPTIONAL COMPONENTS (INCLUDING ASSOCIATED WIRING) (a) Check for optional components. B --  CHECK DTC A -- Continue to next step.

REMOVE OPTIONAL COMPONENTS (INCLUDING ASSOCIATED WIRING) (a) Remove optional components (including associated wiring). NOTICE: Do not remove optional components or associated wiring without the permission of the customer. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

steps.instruction: CHECK DTC (a) Turn the power switch on (IG), and wait for at least 60 seconds. (b) Turn the power switch off. HINT If a communication error occurs, DTCs for both the LH and RH sides will be stored simultaneously. To identify the malfunctioning area, turn the power switch off and then on (IG) again. (c) Turn the power switch on (IG), and wait for at least 60 seconds. (d) Check for DTCs    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes/DTC Check / Clear. Result: HINT * DTCs indicating communication errors will be changed to DTCs indicating errors in initialization by turning the power switch off and then on (IG) again. * Codes other than DTCs B1613/83 and B1618/84 may be output at this time, but they are not related to this check. B --  GO TO DTC B1613/83  See: B1612 C --  GO TO DTC B1618/84  See: B1617 A -- Continue to next step.

(b) Turn the power switch off. (c) Turn the power switch on (IG), and wait for at least 60 seconds. (d) Check for DTCs    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes/DTC Check / Clear. OK:

dtcs.description: Wireless Door Lock Tuner Circuit Malfunction The door control receiver is used to receive electrical waves relating to the entry functions of the smart key system. The certification ECU (smart key ECU assembly) decodes the requested smart key system operation by identifying a key code based on the electric waves received via the door control receiver. The door control receiver receives a signal from the door control transmitter and sends signals to the main body ECU (multiplex network body ECU) through the certification ECU (smart key ECU assembly). The certification ECU (smart key ECU assembly) then sends a command, according to the requested operation, to each ECU (ex. if door lock operation is requested, the certification ECU (smart key ECU assembly) sends a door lock command to the main body ECU (multiplex network body ECU)). NOTICE: * When replacing or inspecting the door control receiver and wire harness, do not change the position or length of the wire harness. If the wire harness is too close to the door control receiver, entry and wireless function performance may be affected. * Before performing the inspection, check that there are no problems related to the CAN communication system and LIN communication system. PROCEDURE

dtcs.description: 11 - Room Temperature Sensor Circuit The room temperature sensor (cooler thermistor) is installed in the instrument panel. It detects the cabin temperature to control air conditioning AUTO mode. The resistance of the room temperature sensor (cooler thermistor) changes in accordance with the cabin temperature. As the temperature decreases, the resistance increases. As the temperature increases, the resistance decreases. The air conditioning amplifier assembly applies voltage (5 V) to the room temperature sensor (cooler thermistor) and reads voltage changes as the resistance of the room temperature sensor (cooler thermistor) changes. This sensor also sends appropriate signals to the air conditioning amplifier assembly. PROCEDURE

dtcs.description: 12 - Ambient Temperature Sensor Circuit The ambient temperature sensor (thermistor assembly) is installed in front of the condenser. It detects the ambient temperature to control air conditioning AUTO mode. This sensor is connected to the air conditioning amplifier assembly and detects fluctuations in the ambient temperature. This data is used for controlling the cabin temperature. The sensor sends a signal to the air conditioning amplifier assembly. The resistance of the ambient temperature sensor (thermistor assembly) changes in accordance with the ambient temperature. As the temperature decreases, the resistance increases. As the temperature increases, the resistance decreases. The air conditioning amplifier assembly applies voltage (5 V) to the ambient temperature sensor (thermistor assembly) and reads voltage changes as the resistance of the ambient temperature sensor (thermistor assembly) changes. PROCEDURE

dtcs.description: 13 - Evaporator Temperature Sensor Circuit The evaporator temperature sensor is installed on the evaporator in the air conditioning unit to detect the temperature of the cooled air that has passed through the evaporator and to control the air conditioning. It sends appropriate signals to the air conditioning amplifier assembly. The resistance of the evaporator temperature sensor changes in accordance with the temperature of the cooled air that has passed through the evaporator. As the temperature decreases, the resistance increases. As the temperature increases, the resistance decreases. The air conditioning amplifier assembly applies voltage (5 V) to the evaporator temperature sensor and reads voltage changes as the resistance of the evaporator temperature sensor changes. This sensor is used for frost prevention. PROCEDURE

dtcs.description: 23 - Pressure Sensor Circuit This DTC is stored when refrigerant pressure on the high pressure side is extremely low (196 kPa (2.0 kgf/cm2, 28 psi) or less) or extremely high (3140 kPa (32.0 kgf/cm2, 455 psi) or more). The air conditioning pressure sensor is installed on the high pressure line. It detects refrigerant pressure to output a refrigerant pressure signal to the air conditioning amplifier assembly. The air conditioning amplifier assembly converts this signal to a pressure value according to the sensor characteristics to control the compressor. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (POWER SOURCE CIRCUIT) (a) Disconnect the air conditioning pressure sensor connector. (b) Measure the voltage according to the value(s) in the table below. (b) Remove the air conditioning amplifier assembly with the connectors still connected. (c) Measure the voltage according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Voltage: HINT If the voltage is not as specified, there may be a malfunction in the air conditioning amplifier assembly, air conditioning pressure sensor or wire harness. NOTICE: * If refrigerant pressure on the high pressure line becomes extremely high during the inspection (if the voltage exceeds 4.88 V), the fail-safe function stops compressor operation. Therefore, measure the voltage before the fail-safe function operates. * It is necessary to measure the voltage over a period of time (approximately 10 minutes) because the malfunction may recur after a while. HINT When the outside air temperature is low (below -1.5°C (29.3°F)), the compressor stops due to signals from the ambient temperature sensor and evaporator temperature sensor to prevent the evaporator from freezing. In this case, perform the inspection in a warm indoor environment. (1) Measure the voltage according to the value(s) in the table below. CHECK FOR A/C SYSTEM LEAK (a) Install the manifold gauge set. (b) Recover the refrigerant from the A/C system using a refrigerant recovery unit. (c) Evacuate the A/C system and check that vacuum can be maintained. OK: Vacuum can be maintained in the A/C system. HINT If vacuum cannot be maintained in the A/C system, there may be a refrigerant leak. In this case, it is necessary to repair or replace the leaking part of the A/C system. NG --  REPAIR A/C SYSTEM LEAK OK -- Continue to next step.
CHARGE REFRIGERANT (a) Add an appropriate amount of refrigerant    See: Heating and Air Conditioning/Service and Repair. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

RECHECK FOR DTC (a) Turn the power switch on (READY). (b) Recheck for the DTC when the following conditions are satisfied. NOTICE: If refrigerant pressure on the high pressure line is excessive, this DTC will be set. Therefore, it is necessary to measure the voltage over a period of time (approximately 10 minutes) because this DTC may be set after the A/C has been operating for a while. HINT When the outside air temperature is low (below -1.5°C (29.3°F)), the compressor stops due to signals from the ambient temperature sensor and the evaporator temperature sensor to prevent the evaporator from freezing. In this case, perform the inspection in a warm indoor environment. Result: NOTICE: If the DTC was set due to an insufficient or excessive amount of refrigerant, the problem may have been solved after performing the previous step. However, the root cause of insufficient refrigerant may be refrigerant leaks. The root cause of excessive refrigerant may be adding too much refrigerant when the level was insufficient. Therefore, identify and repair any refrigerant leaks as necessary. B --  END A -- Continue to next step.

INSPECT AIR CONDITIONING PRESSURE SENSOR (a) Install a manifold gauge set. (b) Reconnect the air conditioning pressure sensor connector. (c) Turn the power switch on (IG). (d) Measure the voltage according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Voltage: Text in Illustration NG --  REPLACE AIR CONDITIONING PRESSURE SENSOR OK --  REPLACE AIR CONDITIONING AMPLIFIER ASSEMBLY  See: Heating and Air Conditioning/Control Module HVAC/Service and Repair/Removal

REPAIR A/C SYSTEM LEAK (a) Identify the area where refrigerant leaks from    See: Heating and Air Conditioning/Service and Repair. (b) Repair the identified area of the A/C system. (c) Evacuate the A/C system. NEXT --  CHARGE REFRIGERANT  See: Heating and Air Conditioning/Service and Repair

INSPECT COOLING FAN SYSTEM (a) Check that the cooling fan(s) operates normally. HINT Refer to Cooling Fan Circuit    See: Engine, Cooling and Exhaust/Cooling System/Radiator Cooling Fan/Testing and Inspection/Component Tests and General Diagnostics/On-Vehicle Inspection. NG --  REPAIR COOLING FAN SYSTEM OK -- Continue to next step.

CHARGE REFRIGERANT (a) Use a refrigerant recovery unit to recover refrigerant. (b) Evacuate the A/C system. (c) Add an appropriate amount of refrigerant    See: Heating and Air Conditioning/Service and Repair. HINT If refrigerant is added and the system has not been properly evacuated (insufficient vacuum time), moisture in the air remaining in the system will freeze in the expansion valve, blocking the flow on the high pressure side. Therefore, recover the refrigerant and properly evacuate the system. Add an appropriate amount of refrigerant, and check for DTCs. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

RECHECK FOR DTC (a) Turn the power switch on (READY). (b) Recheck for the DTC when the following conditions are satisfied. NOTICE: If refrigerant pressure on the high pressure line is excessive, this DTC will be set. Therefore, it is necessary to measure the voltage over a period of time (approximately 10 minutes) because this DTC may be set after the A/C has been operating for a while. HINT * When the outside air temperature is low (below -1.5°C (29.3°F)), the compressor stops due to signals from the ambient temperature sensor and the evaporator temperature sensor to prevent the evaporator from freezing. In this case, perform the inspection in a warm indoor environment. * If refrigerant is added and the system has not been properly evacuated (insufficient vacuum time), moisture in the air remaining in the system will freeze in the expansion valve, blocking the flow on the high pressure side. Therefore, recover the refrigerant and properly evacuate the system. Add an appropriate amount of refrigerant, and check for the DTC. If the DTC is not output, it indicates that the cooler dryer in the condenser is not able to absorb moisture in the refrigerant. In this case, to complete the repair, it is necessary to replace the cooler dryer. Result: B --  REPLACE COOLER DRYER  See: Heating and Air Conditioning/Condenser HVAC/Service and Repair/Overhaul/Disassembly A -- Continue to next step.

REPLACE COOLER EXPANSION VALVE (a) Replace the cooler expansion valve with a new or known good one    See: Heating and Air Conditioning/Expansion Valve/Service and Repair/Removal. HINT Replace the cooler expansion valve with a new or known good one because the cooler expansion valve is either stuck or clogged. NEXT -- Continue to next step.
CHARGE REFRIGERANT (a) Add an appropriate amount of refrigerant    See: Heating and Air Conditioning/Service and Repair. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

RECHECK FOR DTC (a) Turn the power switch on (READY). (b) Recheck for the DTC when the following conditions are satisfied. NOTICE: If refrigerant pressure on the high pressure line is excessive, this DTC will be set. Therefore, it is necessary to measure the voltage over a period of time (approximately 10 minutes) because this DTC may be set after the A/C has been operating for a while. HINT * When the outside air temperature is low (below -1.5°C (29.3°F)), the compressor stops due to signals from the ambient temperature sensor and evaporator temperature sensor to prevent the evaporator from freezing. In this case, perform the inspection in a warm indoor environment. * If refrigerant pressure is not normal after replacing the expansion valve with a new or known good one, the condenser or pipes may be clogged with foreign matter. In this case, clean or replace the condenser or pipes. Result: B --  REPLACE CONDENSER WITH RECEIVER ASSEMBLY  See: Heating and Air Conditioning/Condenser HVAC/Service and Repair/Removal and Replacement/Removal A --  END

REPLACE AIR CONDITIONING PRESSURE SENSOR (a) Replace the air conditioning pressure sensor    See: Heating and Air Conditioning/Refrigerant Pressure Sensor / Switch/Service and Repair/Removal. HINT Since the air conditioning pressure sensor cannot be inspected while it is removed from the vehicle, replace the air conditioning pressure sensor with a new or known good one and check that the condition returns to normal. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

RECHECK FOR DTC (a) Turn the power switch on (READY). (b) Recheck for the DTC when the following conditions are satisfied. NOTICE: If refrigerant pressure on the high pressure line is excessive, this DTC will be set. Therefore, it is necessary to measure the voltage over a period of time (approximately 10 minutes) because this DTC may be set after the A/C has been operating for a while. HINT When the outside air temperature is low (below -1.5°C (29.3°F)), the compressor stops due to signals from the ambient temperature sensor and evaporator temperature sensor to prevent the evaporator from freezing. In this case, perform the inspection in a warm indoor environment. Result: B --  REPLACE AIR CONDITIONING AMPLIFIER ASSEMBLY  See: Heating and Air Conditioning/Control Module HVAC/Service and Repair/Removal A --  END

dtcs.description: 41 - Air Mix Damper Control Servo Motor Circuit (Passenger Side) The air mix damper servo sub-assembly sends pulse signals to indicate the damper position to the air conditioning amplifier assembly. The air conditioning amplifier assembly activates the motor (normal or reverse) based on these signals to move the air mix damper servo sub-assembly to any position. As a result, the amount of air passing through the heater core after passing through the evaporator is adjusted, and the temperature of the air blowing toward the passenger side is controlled. The air conditioning amplifier assembly communicates with the servo through a communication/driver IC and wiring assembly called the air conditioning harness assembly. HINT Confirm that no mechanical problem is present because this DTC can be stored when either a damper link or the damper is mechanically locked. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: READ VALUE USING TECHSTREAM (a) Connect the Techstream to the DLC3. (b) Turn the power switch on (IG). (c) Turn the Techstream on. (d) Operate the temperature adjustment switch. (e) Enter the following menus: Body Electrical / Air Conditioner / Data List. (f) Check the value(s) by referring to the table below. Air Conditioner OK: The display is as specified in the Normal Condition column. Result: B --  PROCEED TO NEXT SUSPECTED AREA SHOWN IN PROBLEM SYMPTOMS TABLE  See: Heating and Air Conditioning/Testing and Inspection/Symptom Related Diagnostic Procedures C --  REPLACE AIR CONDITIONING AMPLIFIER ASSEMBLY  See: Heating and Air Conditioning/Control Module HVAC/Service and Repair/Removal A -- Continue to next step.

(d) Enter the following menus: Body Electrical / Air Conditioner / Active Test. (e) Check the operation by referring to the table below. Air Conditioner OK: Air temperature changes in accordance with each control range. NG --  REPLACE AIR MIX DAMPER SERVO SUB-ASSEMBLY OK --  REPLACE AIR CONDITIONING AMPLIFIER ASSEMBLY  See: Heating and Air Conditioning/Control Module HVAC/Service and Repair/Removal

REPLACE AIR MIX DAMPER SERVO SUB-ASSEMBLY (a) Replace the air mix damper servo sub-assembly    See: Heating and Air Conditioning/Housing Assembly HVAC/Service and Repair/Overhaul/Disassembly. HINT Since the servo motor cannot be inspected while it is removed from the vehicle, replace the servo motor with a new or known good one and check that the condition returns to normal. (b) Check for the DTC. Result: B --  END (AIR MIX DAMPER SERVO SUB-ASSEMBLY WAS DEFECTIVE) A -- Continue to next step.

REPLACE AIR CONDITIONING HARNESS ASSEMBLY (a) Replace the air conditioning harness assembly    See: Heating and Air Conditioning/Housing Assembly HVAC/Service and Repair/Removal and Replacement/Removal. HINT Since the air conditioning harness assembly cannot be inspected while it is removed from the vehicle, replace the air conditioning harness assembly with a new or known good one and check that the condition returns to normal. (b) Check for the DTC. Result: B --  END (AIR CONDITIONING HARNESS ASSEMBLY WAS DEFECTIVE) A --  REPLACE AIR CONDITIONING AMPLIFIER ASSEMBLY  See: Heating and Air Conditioning/Control Module HVAC/Service and Repair/Removal

The air conditioning amplifier assembly communicates with the servo through a communication/driver IC and wiring assembly called the air conditioning harness assembly. dtcs.description: 71 - A/C Inverter High Voltage Power Resource System Malfunction The A/C inverter assembly monitors power voltage from the HV battery in the circuit. It stops compressor control and stores this DTC when the monitored voltage is outside the specified range. The output DTC is memorized as a history DTC. Compressor control may not resume unless the power switch is turned off. /Hybrid Control System/How To Proceed With Troubleshooting OK -- Continue to next step.

steps.instruction: CHECK CAN COMMUNICATION SYSTEM (a) Use the Techstream to check if the CAN communication system is functioning normally. dtcs.description: 72 - A/C Inverter High Voltage Output System Malfunction The inverter in the compressor with motor assembly outputs high-voltage to operate the motor. If there is an open or short in the output circuit, the ECU will stop compressor operation and store this DTC. This DTC will be memorized as a history DTC. The compressor operation remains stopped until both the history and current DTCs are cleared.

steps.instruction: CHECK CAN COMMUNICATION SYSTEM (a) Use the Techstream to check if the CAN communication system is functioning normally. HINT Since the compressor with motor assembly cannot be inspected while it is removed from the vehicle, replace the compressor with motor assembly with a new or known good one and check that the condition returns to normal. (b) Check for the DTC. Result: B --  REPLACE POWER MANAGEMENT CONTROL ECU  See: Power and Ground Distribution/Power Distribution Module/Service and Repair/Removal A --  END (COMPRESSOR WITH MOTOR ASSEMBLY WAS DEFECTIVE)

dtcs.description: Fuel Sender Open Detected This DTC is stored when the combination meter assembly (No. 3 meter circuit plate) detects a fuel sender gauge malfunction via a direct line. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: READ VALUE USING TECHSTREAM (FUEL INPUT) (a) Connect the Techstream to the DLC3. (b) Turn the power switch on (IG). (c) Turn the Techstream on. (d) Enter the following menus: Body Electrical / Combination Meter / Data List. (e) Check the values by referring to the table below. Combination Meter Result: B --  INSPECT FUEL SENDER GAUGE ASSEMBLY A --  REPLACE NO. 3 METER CIRCUIT PLATE  See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Instrument Cluster / Carrier/Service and Repair/Overhaul/Disassembly

INSPECT FUEL SENDER GAUGE ASSEMBLY (a) Disconnect the combination meter assembly connector. (b) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (COMBINATION METER ASSEMBLY - FUEL SUCTION TUBE WITH PUMP AND GAUGE ASSEMBLY) (a) Disconnect the fuel suction tube with pump and gauge assembly connector. (b) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Resistance: NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR OK -- Continue to next step.

INSPECT FUEL SENDER GAUGE ASSEMBLY (a) Remove the fuel sender gauge assembly    See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Fuel Gauge Sender/Service and Repair/Removal. (b) Check that the float moves smoothly between F and E. (c) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. dtcs.description: Exhaust Heat Management Warning Detected This DTC is stored when the combination meter assembly detects a malfunction in the engine coolant temperature sensor that is connected to the combination meter assembly via a direct line. HINT This DTC is for the engine coolant temperature sensor which is used to detect the engine coolant temperature for the exhaust heat recirculation system, not for the engine coolant temperature sensor which is used for the EFI system. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: READ VALUE USING TECHSTREAM (COOLANT TEMPERATURE) (a) Set the vehicle in the maintenance mode using the Techstream    See: Testing and Inspection/Component Tests and General Diagnostics. (b) Connect the Techstream to the DLC3. (c) Turn the power switch on (IG). (d) Turn the Techstream on. (e) Enter the following menus: Body Electrical / Combination Meter / Data List. (f) Check the values by referring to the table below. Combination Meter HINT * The engine coolant temperature value in this inspection is the data sent from the engine coolant temperature sensor for the SFI system. * When the Data List values do not match, a signal output error of the ECM is suspected. OK: Engine coolant temperature displayed on the Techstream is 80 to 100°C (176 to 212°F) after warming up NG --  GO TO EXHAUST HEAT RECIRCULATION SYSTEM  See: Engine, Cooling and Exhaust/Cooling System/Testing and Inspection/Symptom Related Diagnostic Procedures OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (COMBINATION METER ASSEMBLY - ENGINE COOLANT TEMPERATURE SENSOR) (a) Disconnect the G2 engine coolant temperature sensor connector. (b) Disconnect the L27 combination meter assembly connector. (c) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Resistance: NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR OK -- Continue to next step.

INSPECT ENGINE COOLANT TEMPERATURE SENSOR (a) Remove the engine coolant temperature sensor    See: Engine, Cooling and Exhaust/Cooling System/Engine - Coolant Temperature Sensor/Switch/Temperature Switch (Warning Indicator)/Service and Repair/Removal. (b) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Resistance: Text in Illustration NG --  REPLACE WATER TEMPERATURE SENSOR  See: Engine, Cooling and Exhaust/Cooling System/Engine - Coolant Temperature Sensor/Switch/Temperature Switch (Warning Indicator)/Service and Repair/Removal OK --  REPLACE NO. 3 METER CIRCUIT PLATE  See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Instrument Cluster / Carrier/Service and Repair/Overhaul/Disassembly

dtcs.description: Lost Communication with Steering SW The combination meter assembly communicates with the steering pad switch assembly via a direct line. If the combination meter assembly detects a communication malfunction, it stores this DTC. NOTICE: Inspect the fuses of circuits related to this system before performing the following inspection procedure. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: READ VALUE USING TECHSTREAM (MULTI DISPLAY SELECT SWITCH, DRIVE MONITOR RESET SW) (a) Connect the Techstream to the DLC3. (b) Turn the power switch on (IG). (c) Turn the Techstream on. (d) Enter the following menus: Body Electrical / Combination Meter / Data List. (e) Check the values by referring to the table below. Combination Meter OK: Steering pad switch assembly condition displayed on the Techstream changes with the actual switch operation. NG --  INSPECT SPIRAL CABLE WITH SENSOR SUB-ASSEMBLY OK --  REPLACE NO. 3 METER CIRCUIT PLATE  See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Instrument Cluster / Carrier/Service and Repair/Overhaul/Disassembly

INSPECT SPIRAL CABLE WITH SENSOR SUB-ASSEMBLY (a) Disconnect the L52 and z9 spiral cable with sensor sub-assembly connectors. (b) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Resistance: Text in Illustration NG --  REPLACE SPIRAL CABLE WITH SENSOR SUB-ASSEMBLY  See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Clockspring Assembly / Spiral Cable/Service and Repair/Removal OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (SPIRAL CABLE WITH SENSOR SUB-ASSEMBLY - IG2 RELAY) (a) Measure the voltage according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Voltage: NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (COMBINATION METER ASSEMBLY - STEERING PAD SWITCH RH) (a) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Resistance: NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR OK -- Continue to next step.

OK: The operation of the combination meter assembly returns to normal. NG --  REPLACE STEERING PAD SWITCH LH OK --  END

REPLACE STEERING PAD SWITCH LH (a) Replace the steering pad switch LH with a new or a known good one    See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Sensors and Switches - Accessories and Optional Equipment/Steering Mounted Controls Assembly/Service and Repair/Removal. OK: The operation of the combination meter assembly returns to normal. NG --  REPLACE NO. 3 METER CIRCUIT PLATE  See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Instrument Cluster / Carrier/Service and Repair/Overhaul/Disassembly OK --  END Navigation System (for Navigation Receiver Type) NAVIGATION / MULTI INFO DISPLAY: NAVIGATION SYSTEM (for Navigation Receiver Type): B1551, B15A0, B15AD, B15B0, B15B3, B15B5, B15B7, B15BA: HD Radio Tuner Malfunction

dtcs.description: Manual (SOS) Switch Red Indicator Malfunction This DTC is set when the DCM (Telematics Transceiver) detects an open or short in the manual (SOS) switch red indicator circuit of the manual (SOS) switch. The manual (SOS) switch red indicator illuminates for 2 seconds and goes off when the power switch is turned on (IG). If a malfunction in the Safety Connect system is detected, the manual (SOS) switch red indicator will illuminate. However, the manual (SOS) switch red indicator may not illuminate when this DTC is set. HINT The manual (SOS) switch red indicator will not operate to indicate another DTC is set, therefore a voice guidance will be heard. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CHECK DTC (a) Turn the power switch off. (b) Connect the Techstream to the DLC3. (c) Turn the power switch on (IG) and wait for 10 seconds. (d) Turn the Techstream on. (e) Perform "HEALTH CHECK" and check for current DTCs    See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Emergency Contact Module/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/Health Check. Result: B --  INSPECT MAP LIGHT ASSEMBLY (MANUAL (SOS) SWITCH RED INDICATOR CONDITION) A -- Continue to next step.

CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (MAP LIGHT ASSEMBLY - BODY GROUND) (a) Remove the map light assembly but do not disconnect the connectors    See: Lighting and Horns/Map Light/Service and Repair/Removal. (b) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Resistance: Text in Illustration NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR OK -- Continue to next step.

INSPECT MAP LIGHT ASSEMBLY (RED INDICATOR) (a) Disconnect the U3 map light assembly connector. (b) Connect 2 dry-cell batteries (1.5 V each) in series. (c) Connect the positive (+) lead to terminal U3-14 (IND1) and the negative (-) lead to terminal U3-1 (GND1) of the map light assembly connectors. (d) Check if the illumination for the manual (SOS) switch red indicator comes on. OK: Red indicator comes on. Text in Illustration NG --  REPLACE MAP LIGHT ASSEMBLY  See: Lighting and Horns/Map Light/Service and Repair/Removal OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (DCM (TELEMATICS TRANSCEIVER) - MAP LIGHT ASSEMBLY) (a) Disconnect the L160 DCM (Telematics Transceiver) connector. (b) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Resistance: NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR OK -- Continue to next step.

REPLACE DCM (TELEMATICS TRANSCEIVER) (a) Replace the DCM (Telematics Transceiver)    See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Emergency Contact Module/Service and Repair/Removal. NOTICE: * The power switch must be off. * Do not swap the DCM (Telematics Transceiver) with one from another vehicle. NEXT --  PERFORM DCM ACTIVATION  See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Emergency Contact Module/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/DCM Activation

INSPECT MAP LIGHT ASSEMBLY (MANUAL (SOS) SWITCH RED INDICATOR CONDITION) (a) Confirm the red indicator status after the power switch on (IG)    See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Emergency Contact Module/Description and Operation/System Description. Result: B --  REPLACE DCM (TELEMATICS TRANSCEIVER) A -- Continue to next step.

INSPECT MAP LIGHT ASSEMBLY (RED INDICATOR INPUT VOLTAGE) (a) Remove the map light assembly but do not disconnect the connectors    See: Lighting and Horns/Map Light/Service and Repair/Removal. (b) Connect the positive lead of a voltmeter to terminal U3-14 (IND1), and the negative lead to terminal U3-1 (GND1). (c) Measure the voltage. Standard: 1.0 to 8.5 V for 2 seconds after the power switch is on (IG). 0 V when the power switch is off. Text in Illustration NG --  INSPECT DCM (TELEMATICS TRANSCEIVER) (RED INDICATOR OUTPUT VOLTAGE) OK --  REPLACE MAP LIGHT ASSEMBLY  See: Lighting and Horns/Map Light/Service and Repair/Removal

INSPECT DCM (TELEMATICS TRANSCEIVER) (RED INDICATOR OUTPUT VOLTAGE) (a) Remove the DCM (Telematics Transceiver) but do not disconnect the connectors    See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Emergency Contact Module/Service and Repair/Removal. (b) Connect the positive lead of a voltmeter to terminal L160-11 (IND1), and the negative lead to body ground. (c) Measure the voltage. Standard: 1.0 to 8.5 V for 2 seconds after the power switch is on (IG). 0 V when the power switch is off. Text in Illustration NG --  CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (DCM (TELEMATICS TRANSCEIVER) - BODY GROUND) OK --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR

CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (DCM (TELEMATICS TRANSCEIVER) - BODY GROUND) (a) Remove the DCM (Telematics Transceiver) but do not disconnect the connectors    See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Emergency Contact Module/Service and Repair/Removal. (b) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Resistance: Text in Illustration NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR OK -- Continue to next step.

REPLACE DCM (TELEMATICS TRANSCEIVER) (a) Replace the DCM (Telematics Transceiver)    See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Emergency Contact Module/Service and Repair/Removal. NOTICE: * The power switch must be off. * Do not swap the DCM (Telematics Transceiver) with one from another vehicle. NEXT --  PERFORM DCM ACTIVATION  See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Emergency Contact Module/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/DCM Activation

dtcs.description: Manual (SOS) Switch Green Indicator Malfunction This DTC is set when the DCM (Telematics Transceiver) detects an open or short in the manual (SOS) switch green indicator circuit of the manual (SOS) switch. The manual (SOS) switch green indicator illuminates after the power switch is turned on (IG). If the Safety Connect system is not active, the manual (SOS) switch green indicator will turn off. If the Safety Connect system is active, the manual (SOS) switch green indicator will blink while communicating with call center. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CHECK DTC (a) Turn the power switch off. (b) Connect the Techstream to the DLC3. (c) Turn the power switch on (IG) and wait for 10 seconds. (d) Turn the Techstream on. Standard: 1.0 to 8.5 V for 2 seconds after the power switch is on (IG). 0 V when the power switch is off. INSPECT DCM (TELEMATICS TRANSCEIVER) (GREEN INDICATOR OUTPUT VOLTAGE) (a) Remove the DCM (Telematics Transceiver) but do not disconnect the connectors    See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Emergency Contact Module/Service and Repair/Removal. (e) Perform "HEALTH CHECK" and check for current DTCs    See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Emergency Contact Module/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/Health Check. REPLACE DCM (TELEMATICS TRANSCEIVER) (a) Replace the DCM (Telematics Transceiver)    See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Emergency Contact Module/Service and Repair/Removal. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: INSPECT RADIO AND DISPLAY RECEIVER ASSEMBLY (a) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Resistance: Text in Illustration NG --  REPLACE RADIO AND DISPLAY RECEIVER ASSEMBLY  See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Radio, Stereo, and Compact Disc/Radio/Stereo/Service and Repair/Radio Receiver/Removal OK -- Continue to next step.

dtcs.description: GPS Signal Unreceived If GPS satellite signals cannot be acquired for 10 consecutive miles, this DTC is set. HINT If either DTC B15C0 or B15C1 is output at the same time, perform troubleshooting for DTC B15C0 or B15C1 first. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CHECK TELEPHONE AND GPS ANTENNA ASSEMBLY (a) Check that there are no problems with the telephone and GPS antenna assembly. OK: The antenna is not excessively dirty, and there are no objects blocking signal reception. NG --  REMOVE FOREIGN OBJECT OK -- Continue to next step.

REPLACE TELEPHONE AND GPS ANTENNA ASSEMBLY (a) Replace the telephone and GPS antenna assembly    See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antenna/Antenna, Phone/Service and Repair/Telephone and GPS Antenna/Removal. NEXT -- Continue to next step.
CLEAR DTC (a) Clear the DTC    See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Emergency Contact Module/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

CHECK DTC (a) Drive the vehicle for at least 15 miles, manual (SOS) switch red indicator will illuminate at approximately 10 miles if problem still exists. (b) Turn the power switch off. (c) Connect the Techstream to the DLC3. (d) Turn the power switch on (IG) and wait for 10 seconds. (e) Turn the Techstream on. (f) Perform "HEALTH CHECK" and check for current DTCs    See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Emergency Contact Module/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/Health Check. HINT Basically turning power switch off will clear the current DTC until vehicle is driven about 10 miles again with the issue. Result: B --  END A -- Continue to next step.

REPLACE DCM (TELEMATICS TRANSCEIVER) (a) Replace the DCM (Telematics Transceiver)    See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Emergency Contact Module/Service and Repair/Removal. NOTICE: * The power switch must be off. * Do not swap the DCM (Telematics Transceiver) with one from another vehicle. NEXT --  PERFORM DCM ACTIVATION  See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Emergency Contact Module/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/DCM Activation Audio and Visual System

dtcs.description: USB Media Malfunction This DTC is stored when a malfunction occurs in a connected device. NOTICE: After replacing the navigation ECU sub-assembly of vehicles subscribed to pay-type satellite radio broadcasts, registration of the XM radio ID is necessary (w/ SDARS System). PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: REPLACE USB DEVICE OR "iPod" (a) Disconnect the USB device or "iPod" from the No. 1 stereo jack adapter assembly. (b) Turn the power switch off. HINT When this DTC has been stored, it is necessary to turn off the power switch to make it possible for the vehicle to recognize a new device when it is connected. (c) Turn the power switch on (ACC). (d) Connect a known good USB device or "iPod" to the No. 1 stereo jack adapter assembly. HINT * If the malfunction occurred when a USB device was in use, use another USB device for the inspection. dtcs.description: 83, B1613/83: Lost Communication with Front Airbag Sensor RH

83 - Front Airbag Sensor RH Initialization Incomplete The front airbag sensor RH circuit consists of the airbag ECU assembly and front airbag sensor RH. The front airbag sensor RH detects impacts to the vehicle and sends signals to the airbag ECU assembly to determine if the airbag and pretensioner should be deployed. These DTCs are stored when a malfunction is detected in the front airbag sensor RH circuit. NOTICE: After turning the power switch off, waiting time may be required before disconnecting the cable from the negative (-) auxiliary battery terminal. Therefore, make sure to read the disconnecting the cable from the negative (-) auxiliary battery terminal notices before proceeding with work See: Service Precautions. PROCEDURE

dtcs.description: 21 - Side Airbag Sensor RH Circuit Malfunction The side collision sensor RH circuit (to determine deployment of the front seat airbag assembly RH and curtain shield airbag assembly RH) is composed of the airbag ECU assembly, side airbag sensor assembly RH and side No. 2 airbag sensor assembly RH. The side airbag sensor assembly RH detects impacts to the vehicle and sends signals to the airbag ECU assembly to determine if the airbag should be deployed.

dtcs.description: 81, B1623/81, B1632/81, B1633/81, B1642/81: Lost communication with Side Airbag Sensor RH

81 - Lost Communication with Side Satellite Sensor Bus RH The side collision sensor RH circuit (to determine deployment of the front seat airbag assembly RH and curtain shield airbag assembly RH) is composed of the airbag ECU assembly, side airbag sensor assembly RH and side No. 2 airbag sensor assembly RH. The side airbag sensor assembly RH and side No. 2 airbag sensor assembly RH detect impacts to the vehicle and send signals to the airbag ECU assembly to determine if the airbag should be deployed. These DTCs are stored when a malfunction is detected for the side collision sensor RH circuit. NOTICE: After turning the power switch off, waiting time may be required before disconnecting the cable from the negative (-) auxiliary battery terminal. dtcs.description: 23 - Rear Airbag Sensor RH Circuit Malfunction The side collision sensor RH circuit (to determine deployment of the front seat airbag assembly RH and curtain shield airbag assembly RH) is composed of the airbag ECU assembly, side airbag sensor assembly RH and side No. 2 airbag sensor assembly RH. The side No. 2 airbag sensor assembly RH detects impacts to the vehicle and sends signals to the airbag ECU assembly to determine if the airbag should be deployed.

steps.instruction: CHECK SIDE NO. 2 AIRBAG SENSOR ASSEMBLY RH (a) Turn the power switch off. (b) Disconnect the cable from the negative (-) auxiliary battery terminal.

dtcs.description: 24 - Rear Airbag Sensor LH Circuit Malfunction The side collision sensor LH circuit (to determine deployment of the front seat airbag assembly LH and curtain shield airbag assembly LH) is composed of the airbag ECU assembly, side airbag sensor assembly LH and side No. 2 airbag sensor assembly LH. The side No. 2 airbag sensor assembly LH detects impacts to the vehicle and sends signals to the airbag ECU assembly to determine if the airbag should be deployed.

dtcs.description: 43 is stored when a malfunction is detected in the passenger airbag ON/OFF indicator circuit. NOTICE: * After turning the power switch off, waiting time may be required before disconnecting the cable from the negative (-) auxiliary battery terminal. Therefore, make sure to read the disconnecting the cable from the negative (-) auxiliary battery terminal notices before proceeding with work See: Service Precautions. * Inspect the fuses for circuits related to this system before performing the following inspection procedure. HINT * Perform the simulation method by selecting check mode (Signal Check) using the Techstream See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes/Check Mode Procedure. * After selecting check mode (Signal Check), perform the simulation method by wiggling each connector of the airbag system or driving the vehicle on a city road or rough road See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antitheft and Alarm Systems/Diagrams/Diagnostic Aids/How To Troubleshoot ECU Controlled Systems/How To Proceed With Troubleshooting. PROCEDURE

dtcs.description: is stored when a malfunction is detected in the passenger side buckle switch circuit. HINT When DTC B1650/32 is detected as a result of troubleshooting for the airbag system, check the DTCs stored in the occupant detection ECU. When DTC B1771 is output, perform troubleshooting for the DTC. HINT * If troubleshooting (wire harness inspection) is difficult to perform, remove the front passenger seat installation bolts to see under the seat cushion. * In the above case, lift and hold the seat so that it does not fall down. Hold the seat only as necessary because holding the seat for a long period of time may cause seat rail deformation. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CHECK CONNECTORS (a) Turn the power switch off. (b) Disconnect the cable from the negative (-) auxiliary battery terminal.

(b) Connect the cable to the negative (-) auxiliary battery terminal. (c) Turn the power switch on (IG). (d) Measure the voltage according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Voltage: (e) Turn the power switch off. (f) Disconnect the cable from the negative (-) auxiliary battery terminal.

CHECK OCCUPANT CLASSIFICATION SYSTEM CIRCUIT (OPEN) (a) Using a service wire, connect terminals 12 (FSR+) and 13 (FSR-) of connector B. NOTICE: Do not forcibly insert the service wire into the terminals of the connector when connecting the wire. (b) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Resistance: Text in Illustration NG --  CHECK NO. 2 FLOOR WIRE (OPEN) OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK OCCUPANT CLASSIFICATION SYSTEM CIRCUIT (SHORT) (a) Disconnect the service wire from connector B. (b) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Resistance: Text in Illustration NG --  CHECK NO. 2 FLOOR WIRE (SHORT) OK -- Continue to next step.

PERFORM SENSITIVITY CHECK (a) Using the Techstream, perform Sensitivity Check    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning. NEXT --  END

steps.instruction: REPLACE OCCUPANT DETECTION ECU (a) Turn the power switch off. (b) Disconnect the cable from the negative (-) auxiliary battery terminal. (c) Replace the occupant detection ECU    See: Relays and Modules/Relays and Modules - Restraints and Safety Systems/Seat Occupant Classification Module - Air Bag/Service and Repair/Removal. (d) Connect the cable to the negative (-) auxiliary battery terminal. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

PERFORM SENSITIVITY CHECK (a) Using the Techstream, perform Sensitivity Check    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning. NEXT --  END

dtcs.description: Sleep Operation Failure of Occupant Classification ECU In sleep mode, the occupant detection ECU reads the condition of each sensor while the power switch is off. In this mode, if the occupant detection ECU detects an internal malfunction, DTC B1796 is stored. HINT When DTC B1650/32 is detected as a result of troubleshooting for the airbag system, check the DTCs stored in the occupant detection ECU. When DTC B1796 is output, perform troubleshooting for the DTC. NOTICE: After turning the power switch off, waiting time may be required before disconnecting the cable from the negative (-) auxiliary battery terminal. NOTICE: After turning the power switch off, waiting time may be required before disconnecting the cable from the negative (-) auxiliary battery terminal. Therefore, make sure to read the disconnecting the cable from the negative (-) auxiliary battery terminal notices before proceeding with work See: Service Precautions.
