dtcs.description: ABS Malfunction This DTC is stored when a malfunction in the electronically controlled brake system is detected. This circuit consists of the brake booster with master cylinder (skid control ECU) and seat belt control ECU. Signals are sent from the brake booster with master cylinder (skid control ECU) using the CAN communication. HINT This circuit uses CAN communication. Therefore, be sure to check that the CAN communication system is normal before performing inspection See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Information Bus/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/CAN Communication System/How to Proceed With Troubleshooting. This circuit consists of the driving support ECU, brake booster with master cylinder (skid control ECU) and seat belt control ECU. DTC B2085 is stored when the seat belt control ECU detects an interruption in CAN communication between the driving support ECU and brake booster with master cylinder (skid control ECU). HINT This circuit uses CAN communication. Therefore, be sure to check that the CAN communication system is normal before performing inspection See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Information Bus/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/CAN Communication System/How to Proceed With Troubleshooting. dtcs.description: Driving Support System Malfunction The seat belt control ECU receives collision information from the driving support ECU and brake booster with master cylinder (skid control ECU) via CAN communication, and tightens the seat belt by operating the motor inside the front seat outer belt. When the driving support ECU detects a malfunction in the dynamic radar cruise control system and pre-collision system, the malfunction information is sent to the seat belt control ECU, which outputs this DTC. When DTC B2090 is detected, other DTCs are also stored by the driving support ECU (dynamic radar cruise control system and pre-collision system). HINT When clearing the DTCs, first clear the DTC output from the driving support ECU (Pre-Collision 2), and then clear the DTC output from the seat belt control ECU. PROCEDURE

dtcs.description: Ignition Hold Monitor Malfunction This DTC is stored when a problem such as an open in the AM2 fuse, an open or short in the wire harness between the fuse and power management control ECU, a short in the IG output circuit inside the power management control ECU, a short between the power management control ECU and relay, or a short in the relay is detected. NOTICE: * When the power management control ECU is replaced with a new one and the cable from the negative (-) auxiliary battery terminal is connected, the power source mode becomes on (IG) mode. When the auxiliary battery is removed and reinstalled, the power source mode that was selected when the auxiliary battery was removed is restored. * Inspect the fuses for circuits related to this system before performing the following inspection procedure. * The push button start uses a LIN communication system and CAN communication system. Inspect the communication function by following How to Proceed with Troubleshooting See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antitheft and Alarm Systems/Keyless Entry/Keyless Starting System/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/How To Proceed With . Troubleshoot the push button start after confirming that the communication system is functioning properly. * When using the Techstream with the power switch off to troubleshoot: Connect the Techstream to the DLC3, and turn a courtesy light switch on and off at 1.5-second intervals until communication between the Techstream and vehicle begins. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (AUXILIARY BATTERY - POWER MANAGEMENT CONTROL ECU) (a) Disconnect the L135 and L136 connectors from the power management control ECU. dtcs.description: ACC Monitor Malfunction This DTC is stored when there is a problem in the ACC output circuit. The ACC circuit is the circuit that goes from inside the power management control ECU to the ACC relay. NOTICE: * When the power management control ECU is replaced with a new one and the cable from the negative (-) auxiliary battery terminal is connected, the power source mode becomes on (IG) mode. When the auxiliary battery is removed and reinstalled, the power source mode that was selected when the auxiliary battery was removed is restored. * Inspect the fuses for circuits related to this system before performing the following inspection procedure. * The push button start uses a LIN communication system and CAN communication system. Inspect the communication function by following How to Proceed with Troubleshooting See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antitheft and Alarm Systems/Keyless Entry/Keyless Starting System/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/How To Proceed With . Troubleshoot the push button start after confirming that the communication system is functioning properly. * When using the Techstream with the power switch off to troubleshoot: Connect the Techstream to the DLC3, and turn a courtesy light switch on and off at 1.5-second intervals until communication between the Techstream and vehicle begins. PROCEDURE

dtcs.description: Vehicle Speed Signal Malfunction The power management control ECU receives vehicle speed information using 2 methods. It receives a speed signal from the meter ECU via a direct line, and a from the skid control ECU via CAN. If the information sent using these 2 methods is inconsistent, this DTC will be stored. NOTICE: * When the power management control ECU is replaced with a new one and the cable from the negative (-) auxiliary battery terminal is connected, the power source mode becomes on (IG) mode. When the auxiliary battery is removed and reinstalled, the power source mode that was selected when the auxiliary battery was removed is restored. * Inspect the fuses for circuits related to this system before performing the following inspection procedure. * The push button start uses a LIN communication system and CAN communication system. Inspect the communication function by following How to Proceed with Troubleshooting See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antitheft and Alarm Systems/Keyless Entry/Keyless Starting System/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/How To Proceed With Troubleshooting. Troubleshoot the push button start after confirming that the communication system is functioning properly. * When using the Techstream with the power switch off to troubleshoot: Connect the Techstream to the DLC3, and turn a courtesy light switch on and off at 1.5-second intervals until communication between the Techstream and vehicle begins. PROCEDURE s/CAN Communication System OK -- Continue to next step.

dtcs.description: Brake Signal Malfunction The power management control ECU receives brake signal information from 2 sources. It receives a signal from the stop light switch assembly via a direct line, and a signal from the skid control ECU assembly via CAN. If the information from these 2 sources is inconsistent, this DTC will be stored. NOTICE: * When the power management control ECU is replaced with a new one and the cable from the negative (-) auxiliary battery terminal is connected, the power source mode becomes on (IG) mode. When the auxiliary battery is removed and reinstalled, the power source mode that was selected when the auxiliary battery was removed is restored. * Inspect the fuse for circuits related to this system before performing the following inspection procedure. * The push button start uses a LIN communication system and CAN communication system. Inspect the communication function by following How to Proceed with Troubleshooting See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antitheft and Alarm Systems/Keyless Entry/Keyless Starting System/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/How To Proceed With . Troubleshoot the push button start after confirming that the communication system is functioning properly. * When using the Techstream with the power switch off to troubleshoot: Connect the Techstream to the DLC3, and turn a courtesy light switch on and off at 1.5-second intervals until communication between the Techstream and vehicle begins. HINT Check the connector connection to the terminal to make sure that there is no abnormality such as a loose connection, deformation, etc. PROCEDURE

dtcs.description: Power Window Motor Malfunction The power window regulator motor assembly is operated by the multiplex network master switch assembly, power window regulator switch assembly or rear power window regulator switch assembly. The power window regulator motor assembly has motor, regulator and ECU functions. This DTC is output when the power window regulator motor assembly has a malfunction, or the ECU built into the power window regulator motor assembly determines that the fully closed window position has deviated approximately 20 mm (0.787 in.) or more from the normal position. D-Door Motor (Power Window Regulator Motor Assembly (for Driver Door)) P-Door Motor (Power Window Regulator Motor Assembly (for Front Passenger Door)) RL-Door Motor (Power Window Regulator Motor Assembly (for Rear LH Door)) RR-Door Motor (Power Window Regulator Motor Assembly (for Rear RH Door)) NOTICE: *

steps.instruction: PERFORM ACTIVE TEST USING TECHSTREAM (APPLICABLE LOCATION) (a) Connect the Techstream to the DLC3. (b) Turn the power switch on (IG). (c) Turn the Techstream on. (d) Enter the following menus: Body Electrical / (desired system) / Active Test. HINT Perform the Active Test for the power window regulator motor assembly that has DTC B2311 stored in its ECU. (e) Perform the Active Test according to the display on the Techstream.

PERFORM INITIALIZATION (APPLICABLE LOCATION) (a) Initialize the power window regulator motor assembly    See: Windows and Glass/Windows/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Initialization. HINT Initialize the power window regulator motor assembly that has DTC B2311 stored in its ECU. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

CHECK POWER WINDOW CONTROL SYSTEM (APPLICABLE LOCATION) (a) Check that the power window operates normally by opening and closing the window    See: Windows and Glass/Windows/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/Operation Check. HINT Check the power window operation of the window where DTC B2311 has been stored. OK: Each power window operates normally. Result: B --  REPLACE POWER WINDOW REGULATOR MOTOR ASSEMBLY (for Driver Door or Front Passenger Door)  See: Body and Frame/Doors, Hood and Trunk/Doors/Front Door/Front Door Window Regulator/Service and Repair/Removal C --  REPLACE POWER WINDOW REGULATOR MOTOR ASSEMBLY (for Rear LH Door or Rear RH Door)  See: Body and Frame/Doors, Hood and Trunk/Doors/Rear Door/Rear Door Window Motor/Service and Repair/Removal A -- Continue to next step.

CHECK WHETHER PARTS HAVE BEEN INSTALLED CORRECTLY (a) Check that the power window components are installed correctly. OK: Power window components are installed correctly. NG --  INSTALL PARTS CORRECTLY OK -- Continue to next step.

dtcs.description: Glass Position Initialization Incomplete The power window regulator motor assembly is operated by the multiplex network master switch or power window regulator switch assembly. The power window regulator motor assembly has motor, regulator and ECU functions. When the ECU determines that the power window regulator motor assembly has not been initialized, DTC B2313 is stored. D-Door Motor (Power Window Regulator Motor Assembly (for Driver Door)) P-Door Motor (Power Window Regulator Motor Assembly (for Front Passenger Door)) RL-Door Motor (Power Window Regulator Motor Assembly (for Rear LH Door)) RR-Door Motor (Power Window Regulator Motor Assembly (for Rear RH Door)) NOTICE: *

steps.instruction: PERFORM INITIALIZATION (APPLICABLE LOCATION) (a) Turn the power switch on (IG). (b) Initialize the power window regulator motor assembly    See: Windows and Glass/Windows/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Initialization. HINT Initialize the power window regulator motor assembly that has DTC B2313 stored in its ECU. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

CHECK DTC OUTPUT (a) Turn the power switch off. (b) Wait for at least 10 seconds, and then turn the power switch on (IG). (c) Check for DTCs    See: Windows and Glass/Windows/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. HINT Check for DTCs for the power window regulator motor assembly that has DTC B2313 stored in its ECU. OK:

dtcs.description: Driver Side Door ECU Communication Stop This DTC is stored when LIN communication between the power window regulator motor assembly (for driver side) and main body ECU (multiplex network body ECU) stops for 10 seconds or more. PROCEDURE

dtcs.description: LIN Communication Bus Malfunction The main body ECU (multiplex network body ECU) monitors communication between all the ECUs connected to the door bus lines. When the main body ECU (multiplex network body ECU) detects errors in communication with all the ECUs connected to the door bus lines at 2.6-second intervals and 3 times in a row, DTC B2325 will be stored. NOTICE: * When a power window regulator motor assembly is replaced or removed and reinstalled, it requires initialization See: Windows and Glass/Windows/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Initialization. * When using the Techstream to troubleshoot with the power switch off: Connect the Techstream to the DLC3, and turn the courtesy switch on and off at 1.5-second intervals until communication between the Techstream and vehicle begins. dtcs.description: Height Control Sensor Malfunction The DTC is stored when the headlight leveling ECU assembly detects malfunctions in the rear height control sensor sub-assembly power source or rear height control sensor sub-assembly. NOTICE: If either of the following is performed, initialization of the headlight leveling ECU assembly is necessary See: Lighting and Horns/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Automatic Headlight. * Replacement of the headlight leveling ECU assembly. * Replacement or removal/installation of the rear height control sensor sub-assembly or work that changes the vehicle height such as replacement of suspension components. PROCEDURE

HINT If the headlight leveling ECU assembly stores DTC B2450 even though the ECU has been initialized, there may be a malfunction in the headlight leveling ECU assembly. NG -- REPLACE HEADLIGHT LEVELING ECU ASSEMBLY See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Emergency Contact Module/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes OK -- END

steps.instruction: PERFORM INITIALIZATION (a) Perform initialization of the headlight leveling ECU assembly    See: Lighting and Horns/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Automatic Headlight. NEXT -- Continue to next step.
RECHECK FOR DTC (a) Using the Techstream, recheck for DTCs    See: Lighting and Horns/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes/Automatic Headlight. OK:

dtcs.description: Vehicle Specifications have not been Stored The headlight leveling ECU assembly stores this DTC if the vehicle specifications have not been stored in the ECU. HINT The vehicle specifications are stored in the ECU at the factory. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: REPLACE HEADLIGHT LEVELING ECU ASSEMBLY (a) Replace the headlight leveling ECU assembly    See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Emergency Contact Module/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. NEXT -- Continue to next step.
PERFORM INITIALIZATION (a) Perform initialization of the headlight leveling ECU assembly    See: Lighting and Horns/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Automatic Headlight. NEXT --  END

dtcs.description: Height Control Sensor Data Out of Range When Initializing The headlight leveling ECU assembly stores this DTC if the sensor value received from the height control sensor is out of range when performing initialization of the headlight leveling ECU assembly; for example, the vehicle is not level or being jacked up. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: PERFORM INITIALIZATION (a) Perform initialization of the headlight leveling ECU assembly    See: Lighting and Horns/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Automatic Headlight. HINT * Before initializing, make sure that the vehicle condition is proper for initialization. * Be sure to perform the initialization as instructed. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

RECHECK FOR DTC (a) Using the Techstream, recheck for DTCs    See: Lighting and Horns/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes/Automatic Headlight. OK: DTC D2452 is not output. HINT If the headlight leveling ECU assembly stores DTC B2452 even though the ECU has been initialized as instructed, there may be a malfunction in the headlight leveling ECU assembly. NG --  REPLACE HEADLIGHT LEVELING ECU ASSEMBLY  See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Emergency Contact Module/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes OK --  END

dtcs.description: Variation Error This DTC is stored if the headlight leveling ECU assembly for another destination is installed on the vehicle. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: REPLACE HEADLIGHT LEVELING ECU ASSEMBLY (a) Replace the headlight leveling ECU assembly    See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Emergency Contact Module/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. NEXT -- Continue to next step.
PERFORM INITIALIZATION (a) Perform initialization of the headlight leveling ECU assembly    See: Lighting and Horns/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Automatic Headlight. NEXT --  END

dtcs.description: Antenna Coil Open / Short This DTC is stored when there is an open or short in the transponder key coil (built into the power switch). NOTICE: If the certification ECU (smart key ECU assembly) is replaced, refer to Registration See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antitheft and Alarm Systems/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Engine Immobilizer System. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CHECK DTC OUTPUT (a) Clear the DTCs    See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antitheft and Alarm Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes/Engine Immobilizer System. (b) Recheck for DTCs    See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antitheft and Alarm Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes/Engine Immobilizer System. OK:

CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (CERTIFICATION ECU - POWER SWITCH) (a) Disconnect the certification ECU (smart key ECU assembly) connector. (b) Disconnect the power switch connector. (c) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Resistance: NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR OK -- Continue to next step.

REPLACE POWER SWITCH (a) Replace the power switch    See: Sensors and Switches/Sensors and Switches - Starting and Charging/Start / Stop Switch/Service and Repair/Removal. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

CHECK DTC OUTPUT (a) Clear the DTCs    See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antitheft and Alarm Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes/Engine Immobilizer System. (b) Recheck for DTCs    See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antitheft and Alarm Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes/Engine Immobilizer System. OK:

dtcs.description: Communication Malfunction between ECUs Connected by LIN The certification ECU (smart key ECU assembly) monitors communication between all the ECUs connected to the certification bus lines. When the certification ECU (smart key ECU assembly) detects errors in communication with all the ECUs connected to the certification bus lines at a set interval and 3 times in a row, DTC B2785 will be stored. * *: for Canada NOTICE: * If the certification ECU (smart key ECU assembly), transmission control ECU assembly, power management control ECU or ID code box (immobiliser code ECU)* is replaced, refer to Registration See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antitheft and Alarm Systems/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Engine Immobilizer System. * When using the Techstream to troubleshoot with the power switch off: Connect the Techstream to the DLC3, and turn the courtesy switch on and off at 1.5-second intervals until communication between the Techstream and vehicle begins. * *: for Canada PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (CERTIFICATION ECU - EACH ECU) (a) Disconnect the L62, L136, L10* and A24 connectors. *: for Canada (b) Measure the resistance and voltage according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Resistance: Standard Voltage: *: for Canada NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK DTC OUTPUT (TRANSMISSION CONTROL ECU ASSEMBLY) (a) Reconnect the L62, L6 and L10* connectors. *: for Canada (b) Clear the DTC    See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Information Bus/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. (c) Recheck for DTCs. Result: B --  REPLACE TRANSMISSION CONTROL ECU ASSEMBLY  See: Powertrain Management/Transmission Control Systems/Relays and Modules - Transmission and Drivetrain/Relays and Modules - CVT/Control Module/Service and Repair/Removal A -- Continue to next step.

CHECK DTC OUTPUT (POWER MANAGEMENT CONTROL ECU) (a) Reconnect the A24 connector. (b) Disconnect the L6 connector. (c) Clear the DTC    See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Information Bus/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. (d) Recheck for DTCs. Result: B --  REPLACE CERTIFICATION ECU (SMART KEY ECU ASSEMBLY)  See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antitheft and Alarm Systems/Alarm Module/Service and Repair/Removal C --  REPLACE POWER MANAGEMENT CONTROL ECU  See: Power and Ground Distribution/Power Distribution Module/Service and Repair/Removal A -- Continue to next step.

CHECK DTC OUTPUT (ID CODE BOX (IMMOBILISER CODE ECU)) (a) Reconnect the L6 connector. (b) Disconnect the L10 connector. (c) Clear the DTC    See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Information Bus/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. (d) Recheck for DTCs. Result: B --  REPLACE ID CODE BOX (IMMOBILISER CODE ECU)  See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antenna/Keyless Start Antenna/Service and Repair/Removal A --  REPLACE CERTIFICATION ECU (SMART KEY ECU ASSEMBLY)  See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antitheft and Alarm Systems/Alarm Module/Service and Repair/Removal

dtcs.description: No Response from Steering Lock ECU This DTC is stored when LIN communication between the certification ECU (smart key ECU assembly) and transmission control ECU assembly stops for 10 seconds or more. HINT Although "No Response from Steering Lock ECU" is displayed on the Techstream, this message is shown if the certification ECU (smart key ECU assembly) cannot communicate with the transmission control ECU assembly. NOTICE: * Inspect the fuses for circuits related to this system before performing the following inspection procedure. * If the certification ECU (smart key ECU assembly) or transmission control ECU assembly is replaced, refer to Registration See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antitheft and Alarm Systems/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Engine Immobilizer System. * When using the Techstream to troubleshoot with the power switch off: Connect the Techstream to the DLC3, and turn the courtesy switch on and off at 1.5-second intervals until communication between the Techstream and vehicle begins. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (TRANSMISSION CONTROL ECU ASSEMBLY - AUXILIARY BATTERY AND BODY GROUND) (a) Disconnect the A23 and A24 transmission control ECU assembly connectors. (b) Measure the resistance and voltage according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Resistance: Standard Voltage: NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (CERTIFICATION ECU - TRANSMISSION CONTROL ECU ASSEMBLY) (a) Disconnect the L62 certification ECU (smart key ECU assembly) connector. (b) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Resistance: NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR OK -- Continue to next step.

REPLACE TRANSMISSION CONTROL ECU ASSEMBLY (a) Replace the transmission control ECU assembly    See: Powertrain Management/Transmission Control Systems/Relays and Modules - Transmission and Drivetrain/Relays and Modules - CVT/Control Module/Service and Repair/Removal. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

CHECK DTC OUTPUT (a) Clear the DTC    See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Information Bus/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. (b) Recheck for DTCs. OK:

dtcs.description: No Response from ID BOX This DTC is stored when LIN communication between the certification ECU (smart key ECU assembly) and ID code box (immobiliser code ECU) stops for 10 seconds or more. NOTICE: * Inspect the fuses for circuits related to this system before performing the following inspection procedure. * If the certification ECU (smart key ECU assembly) or ID code box (immobiliser code ECU) is replaced, refer to Registration See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antitheft and Alarm Systems/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Engine Immobilizer System. * When using the Techstream to troubleshoot with the power switch off: Connect the Techstream to the DLC3, and turn the courtesy switch on and off at 1.5-second intervals until communication between the Techstream and vehicle begins. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (ID CODE BOX - AUXILIARY BATTERY AND BODY GROUND) (a) Disconnect the L10 ID code box connector. (b) Measure the resistance and voltage according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Resistance: Standard Voltage: NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (CERTIFICATION ECU - ID CODE BOX) (a) Disconnect the L62 certification ECU (smart key ECU assembly) connector. (b) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Resistance: NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR OK -- Continue to next step.

REPLACE ID CODE BOX (IMMOBILISER CODE ECU) (a) Replace the ID code box (immobiliser code ECU)    See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antenna/Keyless Start Antenna/Service and Repair/Removal. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

CHECK DTC OUTPUT (a) Clear the DTC    See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Information Bus/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. (b) Recheck for DTCs. OK:

dtcs.description: ID BOX EEPROM Malfunction This DTC is stored when the ID code box (immobiliser code ECU) detects an internal malfunction. NOTICE: If the certification ECU (smart key ECU assembly) or ID code box (immobiliser code ECU) is replaced, refer to Registration See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antitheft and Alarm Systems/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Engine Immobilizer System. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: REPLACE ID CODE BOX (IMMOBILISER CODE ECU) (a) Replace the ID code box (immobiliser code ECU)    See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antenna/Keyless Start Antenna/Service and Repair/Removal. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

ECU CODE REGISTRATION (a) Register the ECU code    See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antitheft and Alarm Systems/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Engine Immobilizer System. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

ECU COMMUNICATION ID REGISTRATION (a) Register the ECU communication ID    See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antitheft and Alarm Systems/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Engine Immobilizer System. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

CHECK DTC OUTPUT (a) Clear the DTCs    See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antitheft and Alarm Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes/Engine Immobilizer System. (b) Recheck for DTCs    See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antitheft and Alarm Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes/Engine Immobilizer System. OK:

dtcs.description: Engine Immobiliser System Malfunction This DTC is stored when one of the following occurs: 1) the power management control ECU detects an error in its own communication with the certification ECU assembly (smart key ECU assembly)*1 or ID code box (immobiliser code ECU)*2; 2) the power management control ECU detects an error in the communication lines; or 3) the ECU communication ID between the certification ECU assembly (smart key ECU assembly)*1 or ID code box (immobiliser code ECU)*2 and power management control ECU is different and a hybrid vehicle control system start is attempted. * *1: except Canada * *2: for Canada HINT Before troubleshooting this DTC, make sure that no certification ECU (smart key ECU assembly) DTCs are present. If present, troubleshoot the certification ECU (smart key ECU assembly) DTCs first. * *1: except Canada * *2: for Canada

steps.instruction: except Canada

for Canada NOTICE: If the ID code box (immobiliser code ECU)*1 or certification ECU (smart key ECU assembly)*2 is replaced, refer to Registration See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antitheft and Alarm Systems/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Engine Immobilizer System. * *1: for Canada * *2: except Canada PROCEDURE

(b) Check that the connectors are properly connected to the power management control ECU and certification ECU (smart key ECU assembly)*1 or ID code box (immobiliser code ECU)*2. *1: except Canada *2: for Canada OK: Connectors are properly connected. (b) Disconnect the power management control ECU connector. (c) Measure the resistance and voltage according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Resistance: Standard Voltage: NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR OK -- Continue to next step.

(b) Recheck for DTCs    See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antitheft and Alarm Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes/Engine Immobilizer System. OK:

(c) Measure the resistance and voltage according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Resistance: Standard Voltage: NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR OK -- Continue to next step.
